meta.description: FTO algorithms grouped by subset, then by case. Face letters follow the community notation (Sonja Black's 'FTO Notation' doc + Ben Streeter's FTO Notes); {X,Y} bracket rotations, X2 ergonomic doubles and w-suffix wides are supported. Each subset declares the HOLD its algs execute from ('cif' or 'eif').
meta.status: TCP (last layer) imported 2026-07-10; 1L3T (one-look last 3 triples) imported 2026-07-13 from zwegner’s page by tools/import-1l3t.mjs; LBT (last bottom triple) imported 2026-07-13 from zwegner’s page by tools/import-lbt.mjs; 1LP (one-look pair formation) imported 2026-07-13 from the user-supplied sheet by tools/import-1lp.mjs.

TCP.name: TCP — Triangle + Corner Permutation (Last Layer)
TCP.notation_note: The sheet's header says its algs are written in EIF notation, but machine verification (case layer-locality, the filled-slot criterion, recognition structure) shows every alg executes from the CIF hold — matching the community notation doc's description of the TCP set ('presents its algorithms in CIF orientation, then begins many algs with a puzzle rotation'). Verified 2026-07-10; see tools/test-engine.mjs.
TCP.description: Solves the last layer's three triples (corner + two triangles each) and the filled slot in one look, after everything else is done. 18 cases in three groups: Even (even triangle permutation), Odd (odd triangle permutation, needs a starting AUF — cases 7-10's listed algs INCLUDE the AUF; cases 11-12 are written post-AUF, so do the AUF first), and 2-Flip (the filled slot's triangle already matches, i.e. two triples are flipped in place).
sources: Sheet: Edd Dibley, 'Triangle + Corner Permutation (TCP) Cases and Solutions' (New_Notation_TCP_Full_Diansheng_DaYan) — https://docs.google.com/document/d/16KaLlxaUujgYbCgs-QNCbJbA1Tgc23VsbgMrOhl8gig
[2] Ben Streeter, '[FTO] Introduction to TCP + My Algorithms' — https://youtu.be/iyDDCHQn-T8
[3] Ben Streeter, '[FTO] My Odd TCP Algorithms!' — https://youtu.be/NLJbzBDkGzI
[4] A.B. 'Tipster Tickster', 'All of my FTO TCP algorithms and fingertricks!' — https://youtu.be/ohwcpjqFWb0
[5] A.B. 'Tipster Tickster', 'FTO Algorithms' spreadsheet, sheet 'Full l3t' — https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11N8tEu0S8aXDUHAsDVJsrh3Uyr6YGn1Asvse4zfAzus

cases.name: TCP 1
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners and clockwise triangles.

algs.alg: B' R B R'

cases.name: TCP 2
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners and anticlockwise triangles.

algs.alg: {U,BR} R B' R' B

cases.name: TCP 3
cases.recognition: Solved corners, clockwise triangles.

algs.alg: {B,U} (U' R' D R') (U R D' R)

cases.name: TCP 4
cases.recognition: Solved corners, anticlockwise triangles.

algs.alg: {B,BL} (U R D' R) (U' R' D R2)

cases.name: TCP 5
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners, anticlockwise triangles.

algs.alg: {B,BL} (U R' U') (R' D' R U R' D) (R U' R)

cases.name: TCP 6
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners, clockwise triangles.

algs.alg: {B,U} (U' R U) (R D R' U' R D') (R' U R')

cases.name: TCP 7
cases.recognition: Solved corners, clockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: excl. AUF; the listed alg includes it

algs.alg: U {B,BL} (R D R' U) (R D' R U') R

cases.name: TCP 8
cases.recognition: Solved corners, anticlockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: excl. AUF; the listed alg includes it

algs.alg: U' {B,U} (R' D' R U') (R' D R' U) R'

cases.name: TCP 9
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners, clockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: excl. AUF; the listed alg includes it

algs.alg: U {B,BL} (R D R' U) (R D R U') (R' D R2)

cases.name: TCP 10
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners, anticlockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: excl. AUF; the listed alg includes it

algs.alg: U' {B,U} (R' D' R U') (R' D' R' U) (R D' R)

cases.name: TCP 11
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners, clockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: unlike cases 7-10, this text does NOT include the starting AUF — apply it first (machine-verified: the text solves the post-AUF state)

algs.alg: {L,U} U' D (R' U R) D' {L,D} (R' U R U')

cases.name: TCP 12
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners, anticlockwise AUF.
cases.moves_note: unlike cases 7-10, this text does NOT include the starting AUF — apply it first (machine-verified: the text solves the post-AUF state)

algs.alg: {R,BR} U D' (R U' R') D {BR,F} (R B' R' B)

cases.name: TCP 13
cases.recognition: Solved corners, solved triangles.

algs.alg: (R B' R' B) (R' L R L')

cases.name: TCP 14
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners, solved triangles.

algs.alg: {R,BR} (R' U' Rw' R U') (R U Rw R' U)

cases.name: TCP 15
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners, solved triangles.

algs.alg: {L,U} (R U Rw R' U) (R' U' Rw' R U')

cases.name: TCP 16
cases.recognition: Solved corners, 2-swap triangles.

algs.alg: {B,BR} (R' U' R D') (R U' R' D) (R' U' R)

cases.name: TCP 17
cases.recognition: Clockwise corners, 2-swap triangles.

algs.alg: {R,BR} (U Rw' U' R) (U Rw R U') R

cases.name: TCP 18
cases.recognition: Anticlockwise corners, 2-swap triangles.

algs.alg: {L,U} (U' Rw U R') (U' Rw' R' U) R'

1L3T.name: 1L3T — One-Look Last 3 Triples
1L3T.notation_note: zwegner's page dialect, all machine-verified: S/H are the sledge (R' L R L') and hedge (R B' R' B) triggers (S'/H' their inverses), [U]/[U'] are pre-AUF marks executed as the plain move, {X,Y} bracket rotations as in the community notation. Two source algs are transcription errors (they do not solve last-layer states) and are excluded: 6b.E.5's only alg (the case is therefore omitted) and 4b.O.4's fourth alg. Case 1.E.1 is the solved state. Some algs are marked as working-slot variants or as ending one AUF short — hover their note tag.
1L3T.description: One-look last 3 triples: all 179 cases of the Bencisco last layer solved in a single look. Cases are sorted by OLP (where the top face’s stickers are), then triangle permutation (E = even, O = odd), then corner permutation. Set 6c is the TCP set. Sourced from zwegner’s page; algs by Aedan Bryant, with the 6c algs largely from Edd Dibley’s document.

{L,U} U' Rw U R' U' Rw' R' U R'
[U] {B,BL} R D R' U R D' R U' R
[U] {B,BL} R D R' U R D R U' R' D R2
[U] L BLw' U' S U BLw L'
[U] R U R' L R L' U' R B' R B
{L,U} U' D R' U R D' {L,D} R' U R U'
algs.note: working-slot variant: solves this case with the unsolved triangles parked on different faces — machine-verified against its own state, not the pictured one

algs.alg: [U'] {B,U} R' D' R U' R' D R2 U R'
[U'] {U,BL} L BLw' U S' U' BLw L'
[U'] {U,BL} R' U' R B' R' B U R' L R' L'
{R,BR} U D' R U' R' D {BR,F} R B' R' B
algs.note: as printed this ends one AUF short of the case — append U' (machine-verified)

algs.alg: [U'] {B,U} R' D' R U' R' D' R' U R D' R
S U S U S' U' H
[U'] S' U' S' U' S U S'
[U] S U S U S' U' S
H' U S U' S' U' S'
[U'] S' U S U' S' U' S'
[U] S U' S' U S U S
[U'] S' U H U H
{U,BL} S' U' S U' S
{U,BR} S U S' U S'
[U'] {U,BL} S' U S' U H
{U,BL} S U' S U' S'
{U,BR} S' U S' U S

LBT.name: LBT — Last Bottom Triple (Algorithmic)
LBT.notation_note: zwegner's page dialect is plain Streeter notation, all machine-verified: Uo rotations, Uw/BLw/BRw wides, Us slices. 21 algs are written with a leading Uw/Us setup whose restore the page leaves implicit — as printed they end one wide/slice turn short of solved, and each carries a note with the machine-verified closing token (hover the tag). Diagrams show the exact state the first alg solves; the whole last layer is junk at this step, so top-layer content is arbitrary. Trainer scrambles reproduce each alg's exact state, with AUF randomization off (a U pre-turn would change the case).
LBT.description: Algorithmic LBT: the Bencisco method’s last bottom triple (the corner and two triangles of the bottom-left slot, between the F and BL faces) solved in one look no matter where the pieces are. 95 cases plus the already-solved case (the page’s case 21, omitted here), in six sections by corner location, subdivided into groups of four: a case, its mirror, and the same two with the corner flipped. Case numbers match zwegner’s page.
sources: Ben Streeter — the 'trapped triangles' cases (LBT 33, 34, 37, 38) and the traditional flipping-alg cases

algs.alg: BL R' L' R L BL'

1LP.name: 1LP — One-Look Pair Formation
1LP.notation_note: The sheet’s dialect, all machine-verified: S/H are the sledge (R’ L R L’) and hedge (R B’ R’ B) triggers, (U)/(U’) are AUF turns written in parentheses (executed as the plain move), and [Uo]/[Uo’] are whole-puzzle rotation marks — a leading one gives the solution for a Uo-rotated view of the same case, a trailing one annotates the ending orientation (rotations never change the state). Diagrams show the exact state each case’s first sequence resolves. Every sequence is machine-verified as a correct flip sequence: from any state matching its case’s yellow/blue appearance it leaves all three pairs structurally formed, landing in the TCP stage. The parity column and comments are the sheet’s own prose.
1LP.description: One-look pair formation for the Bencisco last layer: form the three corner-triangle pairs in a single look, then finish with one of the 18 TCP algorithms (or 2-look). Pair formation is NEUTRAL (colors are ignored while pairing), rotationless, and ergonomic. Case 6c on the sheet is the already-paired state (omitted here). Solutions are given per AUF and per puzzle orientation; parity notes say whether a sequence preserves the permutation parity.
sources: One Look Pair Formation (1LP) Cases and Solutions, V3 — supplied as 1LP_Rotationless_V3.pdf (transcription: data/sources/1lp-rotationless-v3.json; author attribution pending)

cases.name: 1LP 1
cases.recognition: This OLP has only 11 possible permutations. I recommend learning algorithms for them; they are short and easy to recognize.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: stays — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: H

cases.name: 1LP 2
cases.recognition: There are good solutions here for each AUF, which you can also use to force a 4-move TCP in some circumstances.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: H U' H U [Uo]
(U) S' U' S' U
(U') S U S U'

cases.name: 1LP 3
cases.recognition: You can force a 4-move TCP in this case most of the time, which helps avoid the H' solution.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: stays — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: [Uo] (U) S U
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U) S' U [Uo]
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

cases.name: 1LP 4a
cases.recognition: This case has only one unique solution for pair formation. Rotating to avoid H' is recommended.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: (U) S U [Uo]
[Uo] (U) S' U [Uo']
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U) H' U [Uo']
algs.note: same physical solution as line 1, respelled from the rotated grip its leading [Uo] mark executes — state-identical, machine-verified

cases.name: 1LP 4b
cases.recognition: The mirror image of the previous 4a case.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: [Uo] (U') H' U' [Uo']
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U') S U' [Uo]
algs.note: same physical solution as line 2, respelled from the rotated grip its leading [Uo] mark executes — state-identical, machine-verified

cases.name: 1LP 4c
cases.recognition: Both U and U' AUFs work here; they have been chosen to keep the original permutation state.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: stays — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: (U) S' U S U' [Uo]
[Uo] (U') S' U' S U [Uo]
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U) S U S' U' [Uo']
algs.note: same physical solution as line 1, respelled from the rotated grip its leading [Uo] mark executes — state-identical, machine-verified

cases.name: 1LP 5
cases.recognition: There are many possible solutions here for each AUF, but the ones listed are considered the best for avoiding regrips.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: S' U' S U [Uo']
[Uo] (U) S' U' S' U [Uo']
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U') S U S U' [Uo]
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

cases.name: 1LP 6a
cases.recognition: This case also has only one unique PF solution. Rotating to avoid H' is recommended.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: (U') S U [Uo']
[Uo] (U') S' U
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U') H' U
algs.note: same physical solution as line 1, respelled from the rotated grip its leading [Uo] mark executes — state-identical, machine-verified

cases.name: 1LP 6b
cases.recognition: The mirror image of the previous 6a case.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: (U) S' U' [Uo]
[Uo] (U) H' U' [Uo]
algs.note: solution for the Uo-rotated view of the case — the leading [Uo] mark executes as a whole-puzzle rotation (state-neutral re-grip)

algs.alg: [Uo'] (U) S U'
algs.note: same physical solution as line 2, respelled from the rotated grip its leading [Uo] mark executes — state-identical, machine-verified

cases.name: 1LP 7
cases.recognition: This is three flips away from a completed PF. However you can go straight into the solution without any AUF which is nice.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: stays — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: S' U' S' U' S U [Uo]

cases.name: 1LP 8
cases.recognition: Both the even/odd cases can be forced into a 4-move TCP, but the even permutation is the only case really worth learning.
cases.moves_note: sheet parity column: flips — the author’s own permutation bookkeeping, carried verbatim

algs.alg: H U H U
(U) S' U S' U [Uo']
(U') S U' S U' [Uo]
